# Phase 9.1.5 Non-Activation Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, or deployment authority.

## Objective

Create a final skepticism gate asking whether civilization should ever activate runtime at all, while preserving permanent containment as a legitimate constitutional outcome.

## Scope

- Non-activation doctrine.
- Permanent-containment philosophy.
- Anti-runtime constitutional skepticism.
- Governance restraint doctrine.
- Activation-refusal legitimacy.
- Civilization non-operation philosophy.
- Runtime temptation governance.
- Execution abstinence doctrine.
- Civilization self-limitation doctrine.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No provider execution exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No deployment authority exists.
- No execution-capable infrastructure exists.
- No restraint-review system creates runtime authority.
- No philosophical evaluation bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No non-activation doctrine enables hidden execution escalation.
- No abstinence-governance layer creates infrastructure activation authority.
- No restraint framework weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows blocker evidence and lineage standardization.
- Safe to include now: final skepticism gate and non-activation doctrine.
- Future upgrade: final restraint certification and non-activation closure review.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, API integrations, autonomous governance, or execution systems.
